arts.ttl: X reacts as Tottenham derail Man City in frantic first half
arts.sum: X reacts to frantic first half of Manchester City vs Tottenham Hotspur.

arts.ttl: Tottenham midfielder Yves Bissouma picks up fastest Premier League yellow card EVER as he is booked after just 14 SECONDS for rash challenge on Man City's Phil Foden, breaking Sadio Mane's record
arts.sum: The Spurs midfielder went into John Brooks' book after just 14 seconds after a lunging challenge on Man City's Phil Foden.

arts.ttl: 'That is sublime!' | Maddison second extends Spurs lead at stunned City
arts.sum: James Maddison's second of the game extends Tottenham's lead at Manchester City.

arts.ttl: 'Where has that come from?!' | Maddison gives Spurs early lead at City
arts.sum: James Maddison gives Tottenham the early lead at Manchester City, somewhat against the run of play.

arts.ttl: Rodri parades his Ballon d'Or in front of Man City fans ahead of Tottenham clash after controversially beating Vinicius Jr to the award last month
arts.sum: The Spaniard controversially lifted the trophy at the end of last month ahead of Real Madrid forward Vinicius Jnr, becoming the first Premier League player to do so since 2008.
Manchester City face Tottenham at the Etihad Stadium as they look to close the gap on Premier League leaders Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Tottenham enter race for bargain Man United target who left Old Trafford in 2020
arts.sum: Tottenham have entered the race for Lille’s Angel Gomes and are expected to challenge Man United for his signature ahead of the midfielder becoming a free agent at the end of the season. The 24-year-old has been with the Ligue 1 club since 2020 having departed the Manchester club as the opportunity to play regularly […] The post Tottenham enter race for bargain Man United target who left Old Trafford in 2020 appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Man City vs. Spurs confirmed XIs: Phil Foden, Kevin De Bruyne decisions made, Brennan Johnson benched
arts.sum: Man City and Tottenham confirm their starting lineups for this evening's Premier League clash at the Etihad Stadium, with three changes made by both teams.

arts.ttl: Manchester City v Tottenham: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Live updates from 5.30pm UK time Email Scott | Live scoreboard – all Saturday’s details Manchester City: Ederson, Walker, Stones, Akanji, Gvardiol, Lewis, Gundogan, Bernardo, Savinho, Foden, Haaland. arts.ttl: Akanji, Stones and Bernardo return for Spurs clash
arts.sum: Tottenham have scored at least twice in each of their last three Premier League away games against City, winning 3-2 in 2021-22, losing 4-2 in 2022-23 and drawing 3-3 last season. CITY: Ederson, Walker (C), Stones, Akanji, Gvardiol, Lewis, Gundogan, Bernardo, Savinho, Foden, Haaland
